name: St Cuthbert's Way
description: 62-mile trail following the life of St Cuthbert from Melrose Abbey to Holy Island (Lindisfarne).
key_features: Historic monasteries; Tidal causeway crossing; Celtic Christianity

name: Pilgrims' Way (North Downs Way)
description: Historic route from Winchester to Canterbury following the North Downs. Medieval pilgrimage to Thomas Becket's shrine.
key_features: Canterbury Cathedral; Medieval history; Chaucer connection

name: Augustine Camino
description: Route following St Augustine's journey from his landing at Ramsgate to Canterbury where he established Christianity in England.
key_features: Augustine's landing site; Canterbury Cathedral; Anglo-Saxon Christianity

name: St Columba's Way
description: Route following St Columba's journey from Glasgow through the Scottish Highlands to the sacred island of Iona.
key_features: Iona Abbey; Celtic Christianity; Scottish Highlands; Island hopping

name: Holy Island (Lindisfarne)
description: Tidal island and cradle of English Christianity. Home to Lindisfarne Priory; St Cuthbert's legacy; and the Lindisfarne Gospels.
key_features: Tidal causeway; Lindisfarne Priory; Celtic Christianity; Gospels heritage

name: Iona
description: Sacred island and centre of Celtic Christianity. Home to Iona Abbey founded by St Columba in 563 AD.
key_features: Iona Abbey; Celtic Christianity; Columba's legacy; Iona Community

name: Canterbury
description: Seat of the Archbishop of Canterbury and destination of medieval pilgrimage. Canterbury Cathedral; St Augustine's Abbey.
key_features: Canterbury Cathedral; UNESCO World Heritage; Becket's shrine; Augustine

name: Walsingham
description: Major English pilgrimage site since 1061. Both Anglican and Catholic shrines. Known as 'England's Nazareth'.
key_features: Anglican Shrine; Catholic Shrine; Holy House; Pilgrimage walks
associated_organisations: Shrine of Our Lady of Walsingham

name: Glastonbury
description: Ancient Christian site associated with Joseph of Arimathea and King Arthur. Glastonbury Abbey and the Tor.
key_features: Glastonbury Abbey; The Tor; Joseph of Arimathea legend; Celtic connections

name: Durham
description: Durham Cathedral houses the shrine of St Cuthbert and the tomb of the Venerable Bede. UNESCO World Heritage Site.
key_features: Durham Cathedral; St Cuthbert's shrine; Bede's tomb; UNESCO

name: Camino de Santiago
location: Various starting points to Santiago de Compostela
description: The Way of St James. Multiple routes across Spain converging on Santiago de Compostela Cathedral.
key_features: St James shrine; Pilgrim credential; Multiple routes; International

name: Holy Land (Israel/Palestine)
description: The premier Christian pilgrimage destination. Sites of Jesus's birth; ministry; death; and resurrection.
key_features: Church of the Holy Sepulchre; Bethlehem; Sea of Galilee; Via Dolorosa

name: Lourdes
description: Major Catholic pilgrimage site. Shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes; healing waters; and processions.
key_features: Grotto; Healing baths; Torchlight procession; Assisted pilgrimages

name: Rome
description: Centre of the Catholic Church. Vatican City; St Peter's Basilica; Catacombs; and countless churches.
key_features: Vatican; St Peter's; Sistine Chapel; Catacombs; Papal audience

name: Fatima
description: Major Catholic pilgrimage site commemorating the 1917 apparitions of the Virgin Mary to three shepherd children.
key_features: Basilica; Chapel of Apparitions; Candlelight procession

name: Assisi
description: Birthplace of St Francis. Basilica of San Francesco; medieval town; and Franciscan spirituality.
key_features: Basilica of San Francesco; Franciscan heritage; Medieval town

name: Oberammergau
description: Village famous for its Passion Play performed every 10 years (next 2030). Popular with Christian tour groups.
key_features: Passion Play (decennial); Bavarian Alps; Wood carving tradition

name: Taizé
description: Ecumenical monastic community attracting thousands of young people weekly for prayer; worship; and community.
key_features: Ecumenical community; Youth focus; Contemplative worship; International

name: London Christian Heritage Trail
description: Walking tours exploring London's Christian heritage including Wilberforce; Wesley; Whitefield; Spurgeon; and more.
key_features: Multiple walking routes; Reformation sites; Missionary history; Free churches

name: Christian Heritage Cambridge
description: The Round Church and associated tours exploring Christianity's role in Cambridge history. 20000+ visitors annually.
key_features: Round Church (12th century); University connections; Reformation history
